Message:
Hi, I’m Mary. My husband and I are interested in the property you have listed. We would truly appreciate it if you could share your contact details, as we’d like to have a brief conversation before confirming a viewing.
We hope to hear back from you soon.
Thank you!

Project: openrent-postclosure-corpus
Kind: interaction

Memory:

Source: https://www.openrent.co.uk/messages/41684412
Speaker: Landlord
Seq: 2
Message:
Hi, yes it’s currently still available.

Just to confirm its a second floor 2 bed flat.

Prior to discussing further can you confirm the name of your current employer, length of time you have been there and confirm your salary more than covers rent & bills comfortably.

Please also confirm how many people / who the tenants will be.

Thanks.

Thanks

Message:
Thanks for your interest in renting the property. It is being redecorated and will be available to move in from 18th January. Before booking for viewing, please can you answer the following qualifying questions:
1. Who will be living in the property ?
2. What do you and those living in the property do for work ?
3. What is the total household income? 4. Are you employed or self-employed? 5. If you are self-employed, please confirm that you have been in this position for at least one full financial year. Please also confirm that you will be able to provide proof of your gross annual income in the form of a submitted tax return or an accountant’s reference.
6. Do you have any adverse credit history, including outstanding CCJs or IVAs?
7. Do you have any pets? Please note that only one or two small pets may be allowed under the head lease.
8. What would be your ideal move-in date?
9. Is your current residential address a rental property, and if so, can we obtain a reference from your current landlord or letting agent?
10. ⁠Do you have a guarantor if required.
11. ⁠11. Email address

Hello. I hope this message finds you well.
At this stage, please feel free to send me any queries you may have through OpenRent.

In order to register your interest in this property and arrange a viewing we need to ask you a few questions if that is ok.
Please see the below:

Full name/s (adults):
Number of adults:
Number of children:
Any Pets?:
Occupations of all adults:
Employed/Self employed/ Other?:
Combined annual basic salary/total gross income:
When do you need to move by/earliest you can move?
Any Adverse credit history (CCJs, Bankruptcy, IVAs)?:

If you can get this information over asap that would be great.
Kind regards.
Maria

Speaker: Landlord
Seq: 2
Message:
Hi Mary,

Thanks for your interest in the property; I'd love to arrange this for you. I am not at the flat as it is tenanted. Is it a video of a walkthrough of the flat you want. I can get in contact with the tenant for that.

Before we set that up, I just need to get a bit more information to make sure it’s a good fit for both of us. Would you mind answering a few questions?

1. Why are you moving from your current property and how long have you lived at your current address? Why are you moving from your current place?
2. What’s your occupation, and who is your employer?
3. How long have you been with your current employer?
4. What is your monthly income after tax?
5. When are you hoping to move. It is free the first weekend of April.
6. The house is furnished—does that work for you?
7. Do you smoke or vape?
8 The rent is £1750.00—how will you be covering that (employed income, self-employed income, savings, benefits, etc.)?
9. The minimum tenancy is 12 months—does that suit your planning?
10. Can you provide a guarantor if needed?
11. Are you able to provide references from past landlords and your current employer if needed?
12. Do you have any pets?

Looking forward to your response!

Peter-Frank

1. Please confirm if you and your family will live in the property and not more than 5 people will live in the property including everyone(adults, children) in the family

3. Please confirm that you do not have any pets, and nobody in the household smoke.
4. Please confirm for how long you intend to stay in the property.
5. Please note that tenancy can start only on 1st of August. Please confirm if you are okay with this.

Message:
Thank you for your enquiry.
1. The apartment is fully furnished. There is also the option to have it fully equipped with linens and kitchen ware.
2. There is ample storage solutions. Under the double bed are 2 draws for extra shoes, boots, toiletries etc. There is an ottoman for linens, towels etc. The double door closet has room for storing luggage, ironing board, hanging clothes and shelving. There is a separate shelving unit in the bedroom for books and a dressing table.
3. The bathroom has a utility cupboard.
4. The shower is fitted with a pump making it a power shower.
5. The apartment was a labour of love, renovated by myself to live in.

Please contact Paul. He will help you:
Zero Two Zero Seven, then six three one, then eight seven zero zero

Hi Alice, thanks for coming back.

For everyone’s security, I keep early-stage communication within OpenRent (it helps prevent scams and keeps a clear record for both sides). I’m very happy to answer any questions here before the viewing.

If you can, please confirm:
1. It's just you + your husband who will be the tenants.
2. preferred move-in date, and
3. whether you can make it today, between 18:00–20:00 (and your ETA),

I’ll coordinate with the current tenant and confirm the viewing details.

If your husband has specific questions, feel free to send them here, and I’ll reply promptly.

Regards,
Chris

Project: openrent-postclosure-corpus
Kind: interaction
Source: corpus-all
Entities: corpus-all corpus-conv-110
Tags: corpus post-closure-probe conv:110 speaker:landlord seq:2
Structured: atom=message speaker=landlord seq=2 convId=110 sourceUrl=https://www.openrent.co.uk/messages/42702056
Memory:
Conversation: corpus-conv-110
Source: https://www.openrent.co.uk/messages/42702056
Speaker: Landlord
Seq: 2
Message:
Alice, thanks for the message. Could you kindly let me know the following please?

1. Are you moving in as a couple (thanks for Confimation on this).

2. What is your / combined salary?

3. How old are you / both? (The apartment / block is very quiet with only x3 apartments on this floor - that includes this flat).

4. Do you have children?

5. Do you currently rent and how long at current place?

6. What is reason for you looking for new place?

7. You ok to provide references from current landlord?

8. If issues with affordability (fail OpenRent checks) can you provide a guarantor?

9. How long you seeking the tenancy (short-term or long-term)?

10. What is your move in date? The current tenant is actually buying a new home - so need to check the sale and move out date.

11. Do you have pets?

12. Do you know where the address E14 6NQ - is it suitable for you / both to commute to work? (People have viewed in past and after the viewing saying it’s too far for work - bizarre people don't check this pre a viewing.)

13. Do you both have a UK passport and right to live in UK?

14. Do you have a car?

15. Please confirm you are not an estate agent or pretending to be a tenant to sub-let the flat as a corporate let or serviced apartment? After a viewing.

Subject to the above - I can organise a viewing.

Kind regards,
Jack

Hi, thank you for your inquiry.
Could you please provide the following information:

- Are you looking to rent just on your own or with partner/spouse/child etc?
- ⁠Are you full time employed or self employed?
- ⁠Will you be able to provide earnings payslips?
- ⁠What is your average monthly income (total monthly income if renting with your partner)?
- ⁠Will you be able to provide references from previous landlords
- ⁠For how long would you like to rent the apartment for (minimum time period)?
- ⁠Pets are not allowed in the flat- is it ok for you?
- When are you looking to move in? Property is rented on ASAP basis.

Thank you,
Azamat

Thank you for your enquiry. Please can I ask some questions before I contact the current tenant to arrange a viewing.

What’s your preferred move-in date?
How many people are planning to live in the property?
What is your relationship with any other potential tenants?
The rent is £645pw. How do you plan to cover it? (e.g., regular job income, freelance work, savings, benefits, assistance from someone else, etc.)
Can you provide a guarantor if needed?
Can you offer references from your previous landlord and/or employer if requested?

We look forward to hearing from you.
Kind regards
Nick and Jane

Message:
Thank you for your enquiry about this property.

This is an auto reply to help me gather further information about you to help me consider your enquiry.

Could you please reply to the following pre-screening questions:

The annual rent is £21,600, What is your income for affordability purposes?
What source of income do you have available to pay for the rent?
Do you have a months rent and deposit in advance?
Do you have references? Former (not current) landlord + employer
Has anyone who will be living in the property got a CCJ, IVA, Bad Debt, or subject to bankruptcy proceedings?
Have you ever been evicted?

Why are you leaving your current property?
How long have you lived at your current property?
Who would be living at the property if you were to move in, and how are you related to them?

Do you or anyone that will live in the property smoke?
Do you plan to keep pets in the property, and if so, please provide details?
Would you be able to provide a ‘pet reference’ from a previous landlord?
What is your preferred move in date?
How long do you want to rent the property for?
The property is unfurnished. Is this suitable for you?

Regards

- What is your reason for moving?
- When are you looking to move in?
- How many people will move into the property?
- What is your relation to any co-tenants?

- What sectors / professions are the prospective tenants working in?
- Can you provide past landlord and employer references if required?
- Are you currently living near to the property or in London?
- Is there anything I should be aware of regarding your financial situation and ability to pay rent?

Thank you

Project: openrent-postclosure-corpus

Source: corpus-all
Entities: corpus-all corpus-conv-167

Structured: atom=message speaker=landlord seq=2 convId=167 sourceUrl=https://www.openrent.co.uk/messages/43395399
Memory:
Conversation: corpus-conv-167
Source: https://www.openrent.co.uk/messages/43395399
Speaker: Landlord
Seq: 2
Message:
Simple Questionnaire

1. Are you a UK citizen?

2. If not do you have the right to stay and up-to-date share code with Home Office confirmation letter?

3. Are you renting for the first time? If not please provide the reason for why you are leaving.

4. If rented before can you provide the rent statement for the last 2 years?

5. Is your salary or (combined salaries) if joint tenancies exceed 2.5 x the monthly rent?

6. Can you provide proof of income for the last 12 months?

7. The property is /unfurnished. Is this suitable?

8. Are you a smoker?

9. Are you a student?

10. This property has a garden. Would you prepare to maintain it?

11. Do you have the deposit and one month in advance as required?

12. Can you provide references from the current landlord and employment?

13. Who will move in with you? If over 18, do they also have all the required documentation? Are you one single family and everyone related?

14. If your offer is considered, can we view your current rented property?

15. What is the earliest date you can move in?

16 Will you be able to provide a Guarantor?

17. Are you able to provide any reference, documents and ID, when asked for?

18. Do you have any criminal conviction including driving offence and CCJ?

19. Do you have a pet or intend to have one in the future? If yes, what type of pet?

20. Are you on any kind of benefit taking from UK govt? If yes, please mention.

21. Are you happy to run a credit and reference check?

Finally, if you have any questions please feel free to ask.

Hello,

Thank you very much for your enquiry and your interest in the flat.

Viewings are currently planned for Monday 23rd, and we will be confirming specific time slots closer to the date. If your enquiry is successful, we will get back to you as soon as possible with a proposed viewing time.

To help us check that the property is a good fit, could you please reply with a few details:

Your preferred move‑in date

How many people would be living in the property and your relationship to each other

Your current employment situation (job title/employer or main source of income)

Whether you are comfortable with standard referencing and affordability checks

Many thanks again for your interest, and we look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,

Emily

Message:
Thank you Eva for you Enquiry. Prior to arranging a viewing could you please answer a few more queries:

• Is the property for yourself? If not, explain why you are asking to view for someone else. Please do not apply if you are a rent-to-renter or another intermediary

• How long are you planning to live in this property?

• If you like the property. what’s would be your preferred move-in date?

• Minimum tenancy is 12 months is this suitable are you likely to want to stay longer?

• Where do you live now and why are you looking to move?

• What is your connection to Putney?

• How many people are planning to live in the property, are there any children, if so how old?

• What is your relationship with any other potential tenants?

• The rent is £2750 per month . Is this affordable?
• If YES, What is your net monthly income?
•
• If NO How do you propose to cover it?:
• (e.g., freelance work, savings, benefits, assistance from someone else, etc.)

• Can you provide a guarantor if needed?
•
• Can you offer references from your previous landlord and/or employer if requested?
•
• Have you or any of the other adults ever had any CCJs?.
•
• Have you or any of the other adults ever been in arrears with rent?

Message:
Hi,
Thank you for your message. Please find the details below:
• The property would be for ourselves only my husband and I (not rent-to-rent or intermediary).
• We are planning to stay for around 8 months initially, with the possibility of staying longer depending on our work arrangements.
• Preferred move-in date: Around March end.
• We understand the minimum tenancy is 12 months and we would be open to discussing this.
• We are currently living outside London and are looking to move closer to our workplaces to make our commute easier.
• Our connection to Putney is mainly work-related as it is a convenient location for both of our workplaces.
• Two people will be living in the property myself and my husband. No children.
• We are husband and wife.
• Yes, the rent is affordable for us.
• Our combined net monthly income is approximately £9,000–£10,000.
• We can also show proof of savings if required.
• Yes, we can provide a guarantor if needed.
• Yes, we can provide employer and landlord references if requested.
• No, we have never had any CCJs.
• No, we have never had any issues with rent arrears.
If everything is suitable, we would be happy to arrange a viewing.
Also, if possible, could you please share your contact number so it would be easier to coordinate?
Kind regards,
Eva

Hi there,
Thank you for your interest in my flat in Harrow.
The property will be available from Monday 4th May, and I will be carrying out viewings this weekend. Please note that a 5-week security deposit will be required.
To move things forward, could you please confirm the following:

How long you are looking to rent the property for
The full names of all occupants who would be living in the flat
Whether you have any pets

In addition, please provide the following documents when convenient:

A copy of your photo ID
Right to Rent documentation (if you are not a UK / EU passport holder)
Employment reference
Previous landlord reference
Bank statements for the last 3 months

Once I have the above, I’ll be happy to arrange a viewing or discuss next steps.

Project: openrent-postclosure-corpus

Speaker: Landlord
Seq: 2
Message:
Hi, thanks for your enquiry.

Due to high demand and a large number of enquiries, only applicants who provide full responses to the below will be considered.

Before arranging a viewing, please can you confirm the following:

1. How long have you been renting your current property? (Please note: first-time renters will not be considered)
2. What are your professions, employment status (employed / self-employed / contractor), and annual incomes?
3. Will your credit checks show any CCJs or adverse credit?
4. Can you provide a landlord reference?
5. Can you provide proof of income (e.g. bank statements showing salary deposits)?
6. What is your reason for moving?
7. Do you have a working UK-based guarantor? (This is required)
8. How many people will be living in the property?
9. When are you looking to move?
10. Can all applicants pass Right to Rent checks in the UK?
11. Please confirm your combined annual income meets affordability requirements (minimum 2.5–3x annual rent).

Additional conditions:
• No pets
• No smokers
• Strictly no rent-to-rent applicants or Ltd company lets

Please note, property inspections will be carried out every eight weeks. Any false or misleading information will result in the application being declined.

If you meet the requirements above, please also provide a brief introduction about yourself, including your name(s) and current situation, as some enquiries have been received without this information.

Please also confirm that you are in a position to proceed (subject to referencing) if the property is suitable.

Once I have this information, I’ll be happy to proceed with the next steps.

Thanks

Project: openrent-postclosure-corpus

Memory:

Source: https://www.openrent.co.uk/messages/43342146
Speaker: Landlord
Seq: 2
Message:
Hi there,

Thank you for your interest in my property! To proceed with a viewing, I kindly ask that you provide the following documents to ensure that everything is in order:

1) Number of people who will be living in the property.

2) Current employment details (company name and role).

3) Proof of income, including your last 4 months’ salary slips.

4) Bank statements from the past 6 months.

5) References from previous landlords and employers.

6) Will the tenancy be under a single contract or joint? If joint, please provide payslips and proof of income for the other person as well.

7) Identification: Passport, driver’s license, or other government-issued ID

Once you have these documents ready, please email them to me. After reviewing, I will get in touch with you to arrange a viewing if everything looks good.

I look forward to hearing from you soon.

NOTE:--> DSS/Income Support Applicants: Current employment or benefits details (if you are receiving income support or Universal Credit, please provide the most recent award letter or documentation from the Department for Work and Pensions).

Best regards,
Vishal

Speaker: Landlord
Seq: 2
Message:
Hi Eva,

Thank you for your interest in the property.

Just to confirm, the property is suitable for a maximum of two tenants. There is no living room and we would be unable to change the layout or remove furniture. Usually we have 2 students or 2 working professionals living there because its basically only two rooms.

Before arranging a viewing, I have a few quick questions to make sure it’s a good fit:

- Are you both students or working professionals?
- If working, could you briefly let me know what you do and your approximate annual income?
- If you’re students, could you let me know what you study, which university you attend, your year of study, and whether you have a guarantor or sponsor?

Once I have this information, I’ll be happy to arrange a viewing.

Thank you, and I look forward to hearing from you 🙂

1. What date do you want to move into the property?
2. How many people will be living in the property?
3. How are you related?
4. Does your combined gross annual income total £90,000?
5. What profession/job do all prospective tenants do?
6. Can you provide a guarantor if required?
7. Can you provide past landlord and employer references if required?
8. The property is unfurnished. Is this suitable?
9. Are you a smoker?
10. Do you own any pets?
11. The minimum tenancy length will be 12 months. Is this suitable?
12. What is the reason you are moving from your current property?

Message:
Hi,

Thank you for your interest in 11 Haltside, Hatfield.

To help both of us quickly establish whether this three-bedroom, first-floor flat is a good fit, I’d be grateful if you could answer the questions below. These are asked purely to avoid any wasted time on either side.

• Where are you currently living?
• What is the reason for moving from your current accommodation?
• When would you ideally like the tenancy to start? (The property is available from September 2026.)
• Who would the tenancy be with?
• How many people would be living in the property?
• What is the relationship between any co-tenants?
• The rent is £1,530 per calendar month and excludes bills. How would the rent be funded (e.g. employment income, savings, student funding, third-party support)?
• Do you meet typical affordability requirements, or would you be relying on a guarantor or rent in advance?
• Would you be able to provide a suitable guarantor e.g. UK-based guarantor with property ownership?
• If required, would you be able to provide suitable references (e.g. employer, previous landlord, or academic reference where applicable)?
• Pets are not permitted and the property is non-smoking — would this be acceptable?

All tenancies are subject to satisfactory referencing and Right to Rent checks carried out in accordance with UK legislation.

Is there anything you are aware of that may arise during the referencing process that would be helpful to mention at this stage?

Thank you for taking the time to respond. I appreciate there are quite a few questions, but hopefully this helps ensure the property is right for everyone involved.

Kind regards,
Charles

Message:
Hi Lara. Thanks for the follow up. It's helpful to know a bit more about your situation. Before we arrange a viewing (and our time taken up), I'd be grateful if you could clarify a few points:

1. Could you confirm your full names, please?
2. Whereabouts are your parents based, and what's bringing you to the Hatfield area?
3. Could you share a little more about your employment — employer names, roles, and approximate combined annual income? As a guide, affordability is typically assessed at 2.5–3× the monthly rent.
4. You didn't mention whether you'd be able to provide a UK-based guarantor with property ownership — could you confirm either way?
5. Just to confirm — the property is available from September 2026. You mentioned wanting to move within two weeks of it being available, so that would be mid-September 2026. Is that correct?
6. As you're currently living with parents, could you outline what references you'd be able to provide (e.g. employer, character, academic)?
7. I also asked whether there's anything that might come up during referencing — happy to confirm there's nothing to flag?

Apologies for the additional questions — I appreciate your patience. Once I have these details, I'll be happy to arrange a viewing.

Kind regards,
Charles

Many thanks for your request.
May I kindly ask a few information that allows me to check further for viewing ?

1. May I ask where you work currently
2, What is your monthly take home 3: You will have good references from your current landlord
4. You are okay to submit salary slips for the last 6 months from your current employer once you confirm your interest for the flat
5: Do you claim any benefits 6: You are okay for credit checks 7: your commitment to having a contract at-least for a Year . 8: How many members and is it a family 9: how are you related with the members

Message:
Thank you for your enquiry. I have a couple of initial questions, and look forward to hearing from you with your answers.
1. What is your preferred ‘move in’ date?
2. How long do you plan to stay in the property?
3. What is your occupation and monthly net income?
4. If you will be renting with a spouse, partner or friend, what is their occupation and monthly net income?
5. Will anyone else be living with you, and what is their relationship to you?
6. Can you provide a guarantor if needed? NB: a suitable guarantor is a person who has sufficient income to cover your obligations and owns property in the UK.

Hi,

Thank you for your enquiry.

Could you kindly tell me a little more about yourself before I can arrange a viewing.

What’s your preferred move-in date?

How many people are planning to live in the property?

What is your relationship with any other potential tenants?

The rent is £2,300pcm. How do you plan to cover it? (e.g., regular job income, freelance work, savings, benefits, assistance from someone else, etc.)

Are you in employment?

What is your annual income?

Can you provide a guarantor if needed?

Do you have any CCJs?

Can you offer references from your previous landlord and/or employer if requested?

Do you have any pets?

Many thanks,
Trushna
